- Primary status chip ("Primary" if primary, else "Secondary" for Pro users, or neither for Core where only one exists)
- Pace indicator (per 4.10.6) — prominent
- Goal type descriptor ("Cumulative numeric · Deadline Sept 15")

**3. Primary metric visualization**

Type-specific:

**Cumulative numeric:**
- Progress bar filling from start to target (current_value → target_value)
- Three labels: start, current, target
- Time remaining (if deadline): "42 days remaining"

**Streak:**
- Streak visualization — dots/cells representing days in the streak
- "Current streak: 8 days · Target: 14 days"
- Small muted note if user has had previous broken attempts: "Previous attempts: 3 streaks"

**Best attempt:**
- Bar showing baseline → current best → target
- "Best so far: 295 lbs · 20 lbs to target"
- List of recent attempts (last 5 PRs) with dates

**Rate:**
- Line chart showing rolling rate over the window (30 days typical)
- Target line drawn across
- Current rate callout

**Period count:**
- Progress bar toward target count
- Period window shown: "Oct 1 – Oct 31"
- Current count + days remaining

**4. Pace & history**

- Pace description in one line: "You're 12% ahead of pace"
- Small line chart showing progress over time (the snapshot log visualized)
- Any inflection points noted (goal extended, paused, resumed)

**5. Related activity**

List of data-level activity that has contributed to this goal (last 7 days):

For FIT-01 (weight loss):
```
Oct 14: Logged weight 184.2 lbs
Oct 12: Logged weight 184.6 lbs
Oct 10: Logged weight 185.1 lbs
Oct 7: Logged weight 185.4 lbs
```

For FIN-02 (credit card payoff):
```
Oct 13: Payment detected — $200 applied
Oct 6: Payment detected — $150 applied
Sep 29: Payment detected — $200 applied
```

For NUT-01 (protein streak):
```
Oct 14: ✓ Hit 180g protein
Oct 13: ✓ Hit 180g protein
Oct 12: ✓ Hit 180g protein
Oct 11: ✗ Missed (165g) — streak reset
```

Tap a row → jumps to the relevant category view (e.g., meal log detail, transaction detail).

**6. Actions (bottom)**

- "Edit goal" → Edit Goal Sheet (see 4.10.12)
- "Archive goal" → Archive confirmation modal
- "Share progress" (visible once goal is ≥50% complete) → shares a mid-progress card via OS share sheet (optional; lower-key version of the completion share card)

### Edge cases on detail view

- Goal paused (integration disconnected): primary action is "Reconnect [Source]" instead of edit/archive
- Goal extended: banner at top "Deadline extended from Sept 1 to Dec 1"
- Goal completed: detail view shows completion state with the completion date, "Celebrate" button (re-launches celebration modal), "Share" button, and "Pick next goal" button

---

## 4.10.12 Editing & Archiving

### Editing goals

Users can edit any editable parameter of an active goal at any time.

**Edit Goal Sheet:**
Slide-up sheet opened from Goal Detail View or from overflow menu.

Editable fields (per goal type):
- **Cumulative numeric**: target value, deadline, auto-restart toggle, display name
- **Streak**: target streak length, display name
- **Best attempt**: target value, display name
- **Rate**: target rate value, display name
- **Period count**: target count (period and period_start are NOT editable — period is fixed at creation)

**Non-editable:**
- library_id (can't swap goal type)
- category
- goal_type
- start_value / baseline_value (the initial state is immutable — preserves history)

Changing the target or deadline does NOT reset progress. Example:
- User set "Lose 10 lbs by Sept 1," progress at 6 lbs lost
- User edits to "Lose 8 lbs by Sept 1"
- Progress immediately updates to 6/8 = 75% (instead of 6/10 = 60%) because the formula inputs changed
- No progress data is lost — the goal just recalibrates

Edits are logged in `goal_edit_log` (see 4.10.17) with timestamp, field, old value, new value.

### Archiving goals

Archive is a soft-delete. Active goal → archived state, removing it from the active list but preserving the history.

**Archive flow:**
1. User taps "Archive goal" from detail view or overflow menu
2. Confirmation modal:
   - Title: "Archive [Goal Name]?"
   - Body: "You can restore this goal within 30 days. After that, it's permanently deleted."
   - Primary: "Archive" (destructive styling)
   - Secondary: "Cancel"
3. On confirm: goal status → archived, archived_at timestamp set
4. Freed slot available for new goal

### Restoring archived goals

For 30 days after archiving, user can restore the goal from Goals Dashboard → "Archived" section:
- List of archived goals with archive date
- Tap to view; "Restore" button available
- Restoration brings goal back to active (subject to slot availability; paywall if Core at cap)
- Progress continues from where it left off (no gap-fill for days in archive)

After 30 days, archived goals are permanently deleted (nightly purge job). Data persists in `goal_history` for completed goals but not for archived-and-purged ones.

### Deletion

No direct "delete" action in v1 — users archive, wait, and purge happens automatically. Rationale: reduces accidental-delete risk; archive already provides the "remove from view" function.

For GDPR data-deletion requests (Section 4.11), all goal records are deletable via account deletion or per-category data deletion.

---

## 4.10.13 Failure Handling & Auto-Restart

Goals may not be completed. The handling differs by goal type.

### Cumulative numeric goals

**Behavior on deadline miss:**
- If `auto_restart_enabled = true` (default): deadline silently extends by `original_duration_days`. Progress continues; `extension_count` increments.
  - Example: 90-day goal created Jan 1, deadline Apr 1. User at 6 of 10 lbs lost on Apr 1. Deadline silently extends to Jul 1. Goal card shows "Deadline extended to Jul 1" indicator for 24 hours.
  - No notification fires on deadline miss (silence by design).
  - User can manually archive anytime if they've given up.
- If `auto_restart_enabled = false`: goal status transitions to `expired`. Goal moves from active to expired state. Slot frees.

**Extension limit:**
- Auto-restart can extend up to 3 times total per goal (`extension_count <= 3`)
- After 3 extensions, goal expires silently at the 4th deadline regardless of setting
- Prevents indefinite extension of goals the user has effectively abandoned

**Expired goal UI:**
- Moves to Goals Dashboard → "Past goals" section
- Shows final progress achieved
- Has "Try again" button that pre-fills a new goal creation with same parameters
- Never shown with "failure" framing — just "Past goal: [name], [final progress]"

### Streak goals

**Behavior on streak break:**
- `current_streak_length` resets to 0 silently at next midnight-local after break
- No notification fires
- Goal remains active, user can keep trying
- Goal card shows "Streak broken — starting over" indicator for 24 hours, then returns to "Day 0 of N" display

Streaks are designed to be re-attempted. There's no deadline concept, so no "expiration." Users archive manually if they lose interest.

### Best attempt goals

**No failure mode.** Best-attempt goals are open-ended. User either hits the target eventually or archives the goal. The goal card shows current best vs target; no pace indicator beyond "getting closer" if baseline-to-current shows improvement.

### Rate goals

**No failure mode.** Rate goals are continuously measured. If the rolling rate falls below target, the pace indicator reflects that ("Behind"), but the goal doesn't fail. User archives when they've decided they're done with the goal.

### Period count goals

**Behavior on period end:**
- Period ends (end of month or end of week)
- Status transitions to `expired` (if target not hit) or `completed` (if hit)
- No auto-restart — the period is the implicit deadline and re-running means creating a new goal for the new period

**User re-adding:**
- From expired state, "Try again for [next period]" button pre-fills a new Period count goal for the upcoming period
- Fresh goal, fresh count, fresh period

### Silent fail principle

All failure transitions happen without a notification, without a modal, without any prompt. The user's experience: they open the app, look at the goal, see it's expired or extended. No push telling them they fell short.

Rationale: punishment notifications erode trust and engagement. Users who want to reflect on a missed goal can do so voluntarily; users who missed because life happened don't need Life Dashboard rubbing it in.

---

## 4.10.14 Completion Experience & Social Card

When a goal reaches 100% progress (or hits its completion condition for streaks, best-attempts, rate goals), a celebration sequence runs.

### Completion trigger

Progress calculation (4.10.5) determines completion:

- **Cumulative numeric**: `current_value` reaches `target_value` (direction-aware)
- **Streak**: `current_streak_length` reaches `target_streak_length`
- **Best attempt**: `best_attempt_value` reaches or exceeds `target_value` (direction-aware)
- **Rate**: `current_rate` meets `target_rate` for the full window (30 consecutive qualifying days/periods)
- **Period count**: `current_count` reaches `target_count` within the period

### Completion flow

1. Progress calc detects completion
2. Goal status → `completed`, completed_at timestamp set
3. Slot freed (user can add another goal)
4. `goal_completed` signal emitted (see 4.10.15) — this signal bypasses the normal notification aggressiveness rules and always fires a push (unless master notifications off)
5. Next time user opens the app: Celebration Modal appears

### Celebration Modal

Full-screen, center-aligned. Restrained in tone, not overly festive.

**Contents:**

- Small confetti animation at top (2-3 second loop then fades)
- Large checkmark or goal-specific icon
- Headline: Goal display name (e.g., "Lost 10 lbs")
- Secondary: "Completed in [N] days" or similar
- Stats block (goal-type-specific — see Social Card section below for what appears)
- Preview of Social Card (1/2 scale)
- Actions:
  - Primary button: "Share"
  - Secondary button: "Pick next goal" (opens Goal Picker)
  - Tertiary link: "Done"

Design tone: accomplished but matter-of-fact. "You hit your goal." Not "🎉🎉🎉 YOU'RE A CHAMPION!" 🎉🎉🎉.

### Social Card Specification

The social card is a 1080×1920 PNG generated on-demand when the user taps "Share." Optimized for Instagram/TikTok stories.

**Common elements (all goal types):**
- Life Dashboard wordmark or logo at top, subtle
- Background: dark gradient (dark green/blue palette tied to Life Dashboard brand)
- Headline text (large, sans-serif, bold)
- Stats block (varies by goal type)
- Date range footer (e.g., "Jan 2 → Apr 15, 2026")
- "Life Dashboard" branding at bottom
- No photos, no user data beyond what they chose to commit in the goal

**Goal-type-specific stats:**

**FIT-01 (Reach goal weight):**
```
LOST 10 LBS

195 lbs → 185 lbs
73 days

52 meals logged
28 workouts completed
4.6% body weight
```

**FIT-02 / FIT-03 / FIT-04 (PR goals):**
```
NEW SQUAT PR

315 LBS

Previous best: 285 lbs
Added 30 lbs in 45 days
```

**FIN-01 (Emergency fund):**
```
EMERGENCY FUND
COMPLETE

$5,000 SAVED

128 days
```

**FIN-02 (Credit card payoff):**
```
CREDIT CARD
PAID OFF

$3,200 → $0

11 months
```

**NUT-01 (Protein streak):**
```
14-DAY PROTEIN STREAK

14 FOR 14

Averaged 185g/day
```

**LIFE-01 (Inbox zero streak):**
```
INBOX ZERO
10 DAYS

Zero important unread
for 10 days straight
```

**Template library:**
Each goal library ID has a corresponding template in the app bundle. Adding new goals also adds new templates (bundled as assets; templates are rendered client-side with a shared layout engine).

**Why not server-rendered:**
Templates render client-side because (a) no round-trip latency, (b) no AI cost, (c) works offline, (d) user sees the card instantly. The layout engine uses React Native Skia or similar to render the PNG for export to OS share sheet.

### Sharing flow

1. User taps "Share" on celebration modal or goal detail view
2. Social card PNG is rendered (takes ~200-500ms)
3. OS share sheet opens with the PNG attached
4. User shares to Instagram story, TikTok, Twitter, Messages, wherever
5. Life Dashboard does not track what platform was chosen (share sheet abstracts this)

### Opt-out

User can disable share buttons globally via Settings → Privacy (new toggle added retroactively in v1.11):
- "Show share buttons on completed goals" (default ON)
- When OFF: no share buttons appear in celebration modal or goal detail view

This is a minor privacy surface for users who don't want share prompts at all.

---

## 4.10.15 Goal-Related Signals (4.9 Integration)

Goal lifecycle events emit signals into the notification system (Section 4.9). This is a retroactive edit to the Section 4.9.3 signal catalog.

### New signals (added to 4.9.3 in v1.11)

| Signal type | Priority | Emitted when | Payload | Staleness |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `goal_deadline_3d` | medium | Active cumulative/period-count goal has deadline 3 days away | `{goal_id, goal_name, progress_pct}` | 24h |
| `goal_deadline_1d` | high | Deadline 1 day away | `{goal_id, goal_name, progress_pct}` | 12h |
| `goal_deadline_today` | high | Deadline today | `{goal_id, goal_name, progress_pct}` | 6h |
| `goal_milestone_50` | low | Progress crossed 50% on any goal | `{goal_id, goal_name}` | 6h |
| `goal_milestone_75` | low | Progress crossed 75% on any goal | `{goal_id, goal_name}` | 6h |
| `goal_completed` | high | Goal hit 100% — also triggers in-app celebration | `{goal_id, goal_name, goal_type}` | 48h |
| `goal_behind_pace_sustained` | medium | Pace ratio <0.7 for 7 consecutive days | `{goal_id, goal_name, current_pace_ratio}` | 24h |
| `goal_auto_extended` | low | Deadline was just auto-extended after miss | `{goal_id, goal_name, new_deadline}` | 24h |
| `goal_streak_broken` | low | Streak goal streak just broke | `{goal_id, goal_name, streak_length_achieved}` | 6h |

### Why these signals

- `goal_deadline_*` signals help users actually hit deadlines; they're actionable (user can push harder, or accept they'll miss)
- `goal_milestone_*` are opt-in moments of positive feedback — but they're LOW priority, so they only fire at High aggressiveness. Most users won't see them unless they've dialed up.
- `goal_completed` is a celebration moment; always fires because it's the payoff for the commitment
- `goal_behind_pace_sustained` is a heads-up that something's off — only fires after 7 consecutive bad-pace days to avoid noise
- `goal_auto_extended` is an informational heads-up when a deadline silently extends; low priority, for transparency
- `goal_streak_broken` gives the user the "ah, streak ended" moment without scolding; low priority

### Integration with evaluator

The evaluator (4.9.5) sees these signals like any others. Multi-signal merges involving goal signals are possible:

**Example merge:**
- `bill_due_1d {bill_name: rent}` (high, finance)
- `goal_deadline_3d {goal_name: save_for_vacation, progress_pct: 78}` (medium, finance)

Evaluator composes: "Rent is due tomorrow, and your vacation savings goal has 3 days left — you're 78% there."

### Celebration signal behavior

`goal_completed` fires a push notification even if aggressiveness is Low and user is at daily cap. Rationale: completing a goal is the positive moment Life Dashboard exists to support; interrupting the normal suppression logic is a deliberate product choice.

The push lands; user taps → opens the Celebration Modal.

If the user has already opened the app and seen the celebration before the push arrived, the push still delivers ("You hit your goal: Lost 10 lbs"). Tapping it jumps to the goal detail view.

---

## 4.10.16 Chatbot Integration

The chatbot (Section 4.7) has visibility into goal state via the **GoalsContext** container, specified in 4.7.10.

### What the chatbot can do

- Read active goal names, types, targets, current progress, pace, deadlines
- Read recent progress snapshots from `goal_progress_log`
- Reference goals in cross-domain answers: "How am I doing on my goals?" → pulls GoalsContext + category contexts
- Answer questions about goal history: "What goals have I completed?" → queries `goal_history`

### What the chatbot cannot do

- Create goals
- Edit goals (target, deadline, auto-restart toggle, primary/secondary)
- Archive or restore goals
- Complete goals (mark as done without progress calculation)
- Delete goals

Goal mutations happen only through the Goals System UI (picker, customize screen, edit sheet, archive confirmation, restore flow).

### If the user asks the chatbot to create a goal

Chatbot response pattern (templated in the client-side intent classifier per 4.7.8):
> "I can't create goals from chat. Tap the goals area on your dashboard to pick one from the library — I can help you think through which to pick, or what targets are realistic, if that'd help."

This is consistent with the logging-intent-redirect pattern (4.7.8) where logging actions also don't route through chat.

### Query classification

Goal-related queries are tagged in the classifier (per 4.7.9):
- "How am I doing on my goals?" → query
- "Show me my progress on the credit card goal" → query
- "I want to add a goal for saving $5000" → redirect to Goals Dashboard → Add Goal

---

## 4.10.17 Data Persistence

### New database tables

**goals** — all active, completed, archived, paused, and expired goals. Per the v1.27 unified goal system (§4.10.2), this table stores every goal regardless of category or whether it has side effects on calorie math.

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| goal_id | UUID PK | |
| user_id | FK users | |
| library_id | string | FK goal_library |
| category | enum | fitness/nutrition/finance/time |
| goal_type | enum | cumulative_numeric/streak/best_attempt/rate/period_count |
| display_name | string | Default from library, editable |
| is_primary_fitness_goal | boolean | v1.27 — TRUE for exactly one fitness body-comp goal per user (the one driving calorie/macro math); FALSE for all other goals. Replaces prior `is_primary` column. |
| affects_calorie_math | boolean | v1.27 — TRUE when this goal type can alter calorie/macro targets when designated primary fitness goal (currently only FIT-01 Reach goal weight). Immutable after creation — set from the `affects_calorie_math` flag on the corresponding `goal_library` row. |
| status | enum | active/completed/archived/expired/paused |
| start_value | decimal nullable | |
| target_value | decimal nullable | |
| current_value | decimal nullable | |
| baseline_value | decimal nullable | For best-attempt type |
| best_attempt_value | decimal nullable | |
| direction | enum nullable | increase/decrease |
| deadline | date nullable | |
| original_duration_days | integer nullable | For auto-restart calc |
| auto_restart_enabled | boolean | Default true for applicable types |
| extension_count | integer default 0 | Capped at 3 |
| target_streak_length | integer nullable | |
| current_streak_length | integer nullable | |
| longest_streak_achieved | integer nullable | For history |
| target_rate | decimal nullable | |
| current_rate | decimal nullable | |
| window_size | integer nullable | For rate type |
| target_count | integer nullable | |
| current_count | integer nullable | |
| period | enum nullable | month/week |
| period_start | date nullable | |
| period_end | date nullable | |
| created_at | timestamp | |
| updated_at | timestamp | |
| completed_at | timestamp nullable | |
| archived_at | timestamp nullable | |
| paused_at | timestamp nullable | |
| paused_reason | string nullable | e.g., "plaid_disconnected" |

**Indexing:**
- `(user_id, status)` — primary query pattern for active-goal lists
- `(user_id, deadline)` — deadline signal rule runs
- `(user_id, is_primary_fitness_goal) WHERE is_primary_fitness_goal = TRUE` — partial index for fast lookup of the single calorie-driving goal per user

**Integrity constraints:**
- Partial unique constraint ensuring at most one `is_primary_fitness_goal = TRUE` row per user: `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_user_primary_fitness_goal ON goals (user_id) WHERE is_primary_fitness_goal = TRUE AND status = 'active'`
- Check constraint ensuring `is_primary_fitness_goal = TRUE` only when `affects_calorie_math = TRUE` AND `status = 'active'`
- `affects_calorie_math` is set at insert time from `goal_library.affects_calorie_math` and never updated thereafter

**goal_library** — server-side config, fetched by client

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| library_id | string PK | e.g., "FIT-01" |
| category | enum | |
| display_name | string | Default display name |
| description | string | Short description shown in picker |
| goal_type | enum | |
| data_source | string | e.g., "apple_health_weight," "plaid_transactions" |
| default_parameters | JSONB | Type-specific defaults |
| active | boolean | Deprecated goals have active=false |
| display_order | integer | Ordering within category |
| affects_calorie_math | boolean | v1.27 — TRUE for library entries whose instances can drive calorie/macro recomputation when designated primary fitness goal. At v1.27, TRUE only for FIT-01; FALSE for all other 21 library goals. Server-side config; updatable without app release. |

**goal_progress_log** — daily snapshots for charting

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| log_id | UUID PK | |
| goal_id | FK goals | |
| user_id | FK users | |
| snapshot_date | date | Midnight-local snapshot |
| progress_pct | decimal | 0-1 |
| current_value | decimal nullable | |
| pace_ratio | decimal nullable | For goals with pace |
| pace_status | enum | on_track/ahead/behind/neutral/paused |

Indexed on (goal_id, snapshot_date DESC) for chart queries.

**goal_edit_log** — audit trail of goal edits

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| edit_id | UUID PK | |
| goal_id | FK goals | |
| user_id | FK users | |
| edit_timestamp | timestamp | |
| field_edited | string | target_value/deadline/display_name/auto_restart |
| old_value | string | |
| new_value | string | |

Retained for goal lifetime + 30 days post-archive.

**goal_history** — completed goals (moved from `goals` after completion, preserved permanently)

Same schema as `goals` plus:
- `final_progress_pct`
- `completion_time_days`
- `social_card_shared_count`

Retention: 90 days hot for Core / Pro extends to long-term archived (recent hot, older archived but exportable).

### Migration from Flask `user_goals`

The Flask-era `user_goals` table stored body-composition configuration (Get Leaner / Get Bigger / Lean & Muscular / Stay Where I Am) as the user's single "primary goal" row. Per the v1.27 unified goal system (§4.10.2), that row type is no longer distinct — it's simply a fitness body-composition goal (FIT-01) within the unified `goals` table.

**Migration path (server-side):**

- For every Flask `user_goals` row, insert a corresponding row into the new `goals` table:
  - `library_id` = "FIT-01"
  - `category` = "fitness"
  - `goal_type` = "cumulative_numeric"
  - `is_primary_fitness_goal` = TRUE
  - `affects_calorie_math` = TRUE (copied from `goal_library.affects_calorie_math` for FIT-01)
  - `target_value` = Flask's `target_weight` where present; NULL otherwise
  - `direction` = derived from Flask's goal enum (lose weight → decrease; build muscle → increase; maintain → NULL with `target_value` also NULL)
  - `status` = "active"
  - `start_value` = current weight at migration time
  - `current_value` = current weight at migration time
  - `created_at` = Flask row's `created_at` timestamp (preserved)

- After migration, the Flask `user_goals` table is deprecated and dropped. All downstream code (`goal_config.py` and related modules) reads goal state from the unified `goals` table exclusively.

- Users who had `goal = "maintain"` in Flask (no target weight, no direction) are migrated with no row in the new `goals` table — they start with maintenance defaults and can add a fitness goal post-migration if desired. A one-time in-app prompt explains this: "Your goal has been simplified. Add a specific goal anytime from the Goals screen."

**No separate `user_primary_goal` table exists at v1.27.** Earlier drafts (v1.11–v1.26) referenced a rename of Flask `user_goals` to `user_primary_goal` as a table distinct from the library-goals table. That split is obsolete per the v1.27 unification. There is ONE goals table: `goals`.

---

## 4.10.18 AI Usage in Goals

**Zero AI calls originate from the Goals System in v1.**

- Progress calculation: deterministic formulas (4.10.5)
- Pace calculation: deterministic formulas (4.10.6)
- Celebration trigger: deterministic threshold crossing
- Social card rendering: template-based, client-side
- Goal suggestions in picker: static library content
- Onboarding auto-fill: deterministic mapping from primary goal to library goal

The chatbot consumes goal data but doesn't create or edit goals, so the AI cost of goals is attributed to the chatbot category (Section 4.7.19), not to Goals.

Matches the Settings and Privacy sections in having zero AI footprint. Goals is a tracking and commitment system, not an intelligence layer.

---

## 4.10.19 Core vs Pro Differences

| Feature | Core | Pro |
|---|---|---|
| Goal library access | Full 22 goals | Full 22 goals |
| Active goals (v1.27) | 3 | 6 |
| Fitness body-comp goal drives calorie math | ✓ | ✓ |
| Multiple active fitness goals | ✓ (within 3-goal cap) | ✓ (within 6-goal cap) |
| Progress tracking | ✓ | ✓ |
| Pace indicators | ✓ | ✓ |
| Completion celebration | ✓ | ✓ |
| Social card | ✓ | ✓ |
| Auto-restart | ✓ | ✓ |
| Goal detail view | ✓ | ✓ |
| Progress history chart | 90 days hot | Long-term (hot + archived) |
| Completed goals history | 90 days hot | Long-term (hot + archived) |
| Archived-to-restore window | 30 days | 30 days |

### Core positioning

Core users get the full Goals experience with 3 active goals. They can pick any of the 22 goals, customize fully, track progress, celebrate completion, share socially. The limit is concurrency (3 active vs Pro's 6) and history retention (90 days vs Pro's unlimited archived).

### Upgrade moments

Natural upgrade prompts appear when:
- Core user tries to add a 4th active goal (paywall)
- Core user completes a goal and is clearly goal-oriented (celebration modal includes "Pro lets you track up to 6 goals at once" footer)
- Core user scrolls past 90 days in progress history (prompt to "See full history with Pro")

These prompts are low-key and appear only in contexts where the user is already engaging with the feature.

---

## 4.10.20 Friction-Reduction Commitments

| Potential Friction | Reduction Applied |
|---|---|
| Open-ended goal text leading to untrackable goals | Removed — library-only picker |
| Manual progress updates required | Removed — automatic from category data |
| Complex goal configuration UI | Reduced — one customize screen with defaults pre-filled per goal type |
| Goal failure producing shame / punishment | Removed — silent fail, auto-restart for applicable goals |
| Deadline passing without the user realizing | Addressed — deadline_3d / 1d / today signals |
| Lost progress when goal auto-restarts | Preserved — extension preserves progress and existing start_value |
| Accidental goal deletion | Reduced — archive → 30-day restore window → permanent delete |
| Cannot restore an archived goal after 30 days | Managed — explicit notice at archive time; users can re-add from library |
| Changing target value resets progress | Removed — progress is recalculated from current data without loss |
| Primary vs secondary goal confusion | Resolved — clear labels, drag-to-reorder on Goals Dashboard |
| Over-ambitious users setting too many goals | Enforced — hard cap per tier |
| Goals for features Life Dashboard doesn't track | Excluded from library — not shown as options |
| Pro paywall in mid-goal-creation flow | Shown early — before picker populates for Core at cap |
| "What do I do with a missed goal?" anxiety | Addressed — silent fail + auto-restart for applicable types; "Past goals" section shows expired goals without shame framing |
| Celebration feeling performative when goal was easy | Managed — stats block emphasizes duration and effort, not just "you did it" |
| Social sharing feeling required | Managed — "Share" is optional; "Done" is always available |
| Unclear why a goal is "paused" | Explained — banner text shows integration disconnection as the cause |
| Users wanting to track goals Life Dashboard can't measure | Framed — library shows what's possible; deferred goals noted in 4.10.3 |

---

## 4.10.21 Accessibility Requirements

- All goal library rows have accessibility labels that include name + category ("Reach goal weight, Fitness")
- Goal cards on Home Dashboard, category views, and Goals Dashboard announce: goal name + progress percentage + pace status ("Reach goal weight, 40% complete, on track")
- Pace indicators use color + text — never color alone
- Progress bars have semantic accessibility roles with percentage announcements
- Celebration modal is fully accessible: focus moves to the headline on open; actions are reachable via keyboard/VoiceOver navigation; confetti animation has `aria-hidden` to avoid noise
- Social card PNG includes a textual alt-description for the shared image if the share target supports it
- Edit Goal Sheet uses standard form semantics
- Archive confirmation modal is navigable with assistive tech
- Font scaling: all goal cards, detail views, and celebration screens respect OS Dynamic Type; no text truncation at 200% scale
- Deadline date pickers use OS-native components (fully accessible)
- Streak visualizations (dot arrays) have alt descriptions: "Streak: 8 of 14 days complete"

---

## 4.10.22 Edge Cases & Error Handling

**User has active goal and downgrades from Pro to Core with 3 goals:**
- No immediate archive enforced
- Grace period of 30 days, during which all 3 goals remain active
- After 30 days, the 2 non-primary goals auto-archive (restorable within next 30 days if re-upgraded)
- User sees banner: "Your extra goals will be archived in X days. Upgrade to Pro to keep all 3."

**User hits target exactly at deadline:**
- Goal completes; no auto-restart triggered (completion takes precedence over expiration)

**User overshoots target significantly (e.g., loses 15 lbs when target was 10):**
- Goal completes at 100% progress (capped at 1.0 for display)
- Celebration fires
- No separate "exceeded target" indicator in v1 (deferred to v1.1+ as a nice-to-have)

**User deletes source data (e.g., all meal logs) while goal is active:**
- Nightly progress recalc updates to new (lower) state
- Pace indicator reflects the new reality
- No special handling — the data is what it is

**User disconnects an integration that powers an active goal:**
- Goal status → `paused`
- Banner shown: "Reconnect Apple Health to continue tracking Reach goal weight"
- Goal remains in active list (counts against slot) until user either reconnects or archives
- Pause doesn't count toward extension count

**User connects a new integration that could auto-populate historical goal data (e.g., connects Apple Health mid-goal):**
- Goal uses data going forward only; no backfill of pre-goal-creation data
- The `start_value` is fixed at creation; connecting new sources doesn't alter it

**User reaches extension_count=3 and hits deadline again:**
- Goal expires silently (no extension)
- User can re-add the goal from the library (starts fresh)

**User's timezone changes and a period-count goal's period is mid-span:**
- Period boundaries use goal-creation-time timezone for the entire life of the goal
- Prevents period-length inconsistency from timezone shifts

**Two goals share the same underlying data and compete (e.g., "Lose 10 lbs" + "Gain 10 lbs"):**
- Both tracked simultaneously; progress moves opposite directions from the same weight data
- No conflict-detection logic — this is a user choice, not a system state
- In practice rare; the library's goal descriptions imply mutual exclusivity

**User creates a goal with deadline in the past:**
- Picker/Customize screen validation prevents this — deadline input accepts only today or future dates

**User creates a cumulative goal with target equal to start (e.g., target weight == current weight):**
- Picker/Customize screen prevents this — target must differ from current by at least a minimum threshold

**User's primary goal completes; no other goals active:**
- Celebration fires
- Home Dashboard goal strip hides (no active goals)
- Next time user opens Goals Dashboard, "Pick a new goal" prompt is prominent

**User's primary goal is archived while secondary exists:**
- The oldest secondary (by created_at) automatically promotes to primary
- User sees toast: "[Secondary Goal] is now your primary goal"

**Deep-link from notification points to a goal that's been archived:**
- App opens to Goals Dashboard → Archived section, highlighting that goal
- User can restore or view history

**Race condition: two devices complete the goal simultaneously:**
- Server-side lock ensures only one completion event fires
- Audit log captures the first-to-complete; other device's completion event is a no-op

**Onboarding: user selects a library goal that requires integration data not yet connected:**
- Warning shown on goal customization: "This goal needs [Integration] to track automatically. You can connect it next step." (Refers to Connections step 4.1.7)
- Goal created anyway; pauses until integration connected
- If user doesn't connect, goal remains paused

**Goal completes same day it was auto-extended:**
- Completion takes precedence; celebration fires; extension indicator hidden

**User edits a goal's target downward after making progress (intentional scaling-back):**
- New progress percentage may immediately jump to 100%
- If it does, goal auto-completes; celebration fires
- This is allowed — user is choosing to acknowledge "this is where I wanted to be"

---

## 4.10.23 Analytics Events

All events fired with `user_id` (anonymized), `timestamp`, `app_version`, and `tier`.

**Goal lifecycle**
- `goal_picker_opened` (source: goals_dashboard | category_view | onboarding)
- `goal_picker_category_tapped` (category)
- `goal_library_item_tapped` (library_id)
- `goal_customize_shown` (library_id)
- `goal_created` (library_id, goal_type, is_primary, has_deadline, auto_restart_enabled)
- `goal_creation_cancelled` (library_id, from_step: picker | customize)
- `goal_edit_opened` (goal_id)
- `goal_edited` (goal_id, field_edited)
- `goal_archived` (goal_id, status_before_archive, final_progress_pct)
- `goal_restored` (goal_id, days_in_archive)
- `goal_primary_swapped` (goal_id_promoted, goal_id_demoted)
- `goal_order_changed` (goal_id, new_position)
- `goal_paused` (goal_id, reason)
- `goal_resumed` (goal_id, days_paused)
- `goal_auto_extended` (goal_id, extension_count_after, new_deadline)
- `goal_expired` (goal_id, final_progress_pct, extension_count)
- `goal_completed` (goal_id, goal_type, days_to_complete, final_value)

**Detail & interaction**
- `goal_detail_viewed` (goal_id)
- `goal_history_viewed` (goal_id)
- `goal_related_activity_tapped` (goal_id, activity_type)

**Celebration & social**
- `celebration_shown` (goal_id)
- `celebration_dismissed` (goal_id, time_on_screen_ms)
- `celebration_share_tapped` (goal_id)
- `social_card_rendered` (goal_id, template_variant)
- `social_card_shared` (goal_id) — fires when OS share sheet is presented
- `celebration_pick_next_goal_tapped` (goal_id)

**Onboarding integration**
- `onboarding_goal_step_shown`
- `onboarding_goal_accepted_prefill` (library_id)
- `onboarding_goal_customized` (library_id)
- `onboarding_goal_browsed_library`
- `onboarding_goal_skipped`

**Paywall interactions**
- `goal_paywall_shown` (trigger: at_cap | lite_secondary_attempt)
- `goal_paywall_upgraded`

**Goals Dashboard**
- `goals_dashboard_opened`
- `goals_dashboard_add_tapped`
- `goals_dashboard_tab_switched` (active | past | archived)

Events never include the raw target values or amounts — those stay out of analytics to respect the sensitive-data-in-analytics principle. Only metadata: goal type, category, library_id, progress_pct (rounded to nearest 10%), days-to-complete.

---

# Section 4.11 — Data Export & Account

## 4.11.1 Overview

Data Export & Account is the closing surface of the product — the flows where users take their data with them, shut down their account, or return after changing their mind. It is the infrastructure that makes Life Dashboard's privacy promises real: users can walk away with everything, and when they choose to leave, the departure is complete and verifiable.

### Philosophy

Three principles govern this section:

1. **Data is the user's, not the platform's.** Export must be comprehensive, free, self-service, and work regardless of subscription state. Users who pay us to help them organize their lives get to take that data when they're done.
2. **Deletion must be complete and actually complete.** When a user deletes their account, every trace of their data is purged from Life Dashboard's systems and from every third-party integration Life Dashboard established on their behalf. No zombie data, no lingering OAuth tokens, no "we kept this for analytics."
3. **The grace period is real, but the end is final.** 30 days to reconsider — then permanent. No exceptions, no manual restore after day 30. Users who want safety can export before deletion; users who miss the window accept the consequences.

### What This Section Is Not

- Not per-category data deletion (that's 4.8.7 Privacy — "Delete all meals," etc.)
- Not per-audit-trail deletion (chatbot audit in 4.7.15, notification audit in 4.9.15)
- Not subscription management (that's 4.8.5)
- Not sign-out (that's 4.8.11)

### Success Metrics

- **Export completion rate** — percentage of initiated exports that successfully deliver to the user's email. Target: 99%+ within 24 hours.
- **Deletion completion rate** — percentage of initiated deletions that complete the 30-day cycle and purge successfully. Target: 99%+.
- **Restore rate** — percentage of deletion initiations that are cancelled within grace period. A moderate rate (10-20%) suggests healthy second-thought behavior; very low rates could indicate users being unable to find the restore path; very high rates could indicate the deletion flow being too easy to trigger accidentally.
- **Support escalations related to export** — target <1% of exports generate a support ticket.
- **Support escalations related to deletion** — target <0.5% of deletions generate a support ticket (mostly for legal/GDPR clarifications).

---

## 4.11.2 Scope Boundary

This section owns the flows and mechanics for full-account operations. Other surfaces own partial operations:

| Operation | Owned by |
|---|---|
| Export all account data | 4.11 (this section) |
| Delete entire account | 4.11 (this section) |
| Delete all meal history | 4.8.7 Privacy |
| Delete all workout history | 4.8.7 Privacy |
| Delete all location history | 4.8.7 Privacy |
| Delete a specific chatbot audit record | 4.7.15 |
| Delete a specific notification audit record | 4.9.15 |
| Archive a goal | 4.10.12 |
| Disconnect a single integration (+ 30-day source-specific purge) | 4.8.6 |
| Sign out (session termination, no data impact) | 4.8.11 |

Cross-references are preserved throughout this section. When deletion runs (4.11.13), it supersedes any in-progress partial deletion — if a user had initiated "Delete all meals" a day before deleting their account, both purges complete, but the account-deletion purge is the binding one.

---

## 4.11.3 Data Export — Design Principles

### GDPR Article 15 Framing

The Right of Access under GDPR Article 15 requires:
- Data provided within 30 days of request
- First request free; repeated frequent requests can incur reasonable fees
- Data in a "commonly used and machine-readable format"
- Access must not be conditioned on payment or subscription tier

Life Dashboard exceeds the 30-day requirement significantly (target: within 24 hours for most accounts). Export is free for all tiers. Format is CSV + JSON — both commonly used and machine-readable.

This isn't charity. It's the legal baseline we're committing to meet without friction. Users who want their data get it.

### Tier Gating

**Export is available to all tiers.** Not Pro-gated, not rate-limited by plan, not feature-downgraded for Core.

This is a deliberate reversal of an earlier Pro-gating commitment. Reasoning:
- GDPR Article 15 conflict — requiring Pro subscription to access personal data is a dark pattern with regulatory exposure in the EU
- Support ops cost of manual export fulfillment (the compliant-alternative pattern) exceeds the marginal value of Pro-gating
- Pro has sufficient differentiation without export (higher daily chatbot quota, High notification aggressiveness, long-term history with hot+archived tiering, unlimited email and calendar accounts beyond Core's 2-each limit, AI email drafts, advanced pattern analysis)

Core and Pro users see the same Export My Data row, initiate the same flow, and receive the same output.

### Self-Service

Users generate exports directly from Settings — no support ticket, no email-in, no 30-day-wait boilerplate. Self-service export scales operationally and matches modern consumer product expectations.

### Non-Destructive

Generating an export never modifies or deletes user data. The export is a snapshot; the live data remains unchanged. Users can generate exports repeatedly (subject to rate limits) without data loss.

---

## 4.11.4 Export Contents

The export is comprehensive: every piece of data Life Dashboard has captured or derived about the user that is meaningful to them. Certain categories are excluded for security or utility reasons (detailed below).

### Included in export

**User & profile**
- `user.json` — email, name, plan tier, account created_at, account state
- `user_primary_goal.json` — onboarding primary goal (nutrition engine config)
- `user_preferences.json` — theme, units, language, timezone, haptics, app lock state
- `user_ai_consent.json` — per-data-type AI context consent state
- `user_macro_locks.json` — macro override values and lock states
- `user_onboarding.json` — raw onboarding answers (Steps 1-3)
- `user_archetype.json` — AI-generated archetype name + personalized insight + generation timestamp

**Nutrition**
- `meals.csv` + `meals.json` — every logged meal with: timestamp, description, source (manual/voice/photo/barcode/saved), nutrition values (calories/protein/carbs/fat/sugar/fiber/sodium), image filename (image not included — see exclusions)
- `saved_meals.csv` — user-saved meal templates
- `custom_products.csv` — custom pantry items user has created
- `hydration_logs.csv` — water logs with timestamp and volume

**Fitness**
- `body_weight_logs.csv` — weight entries with timestamp, value, source (manual/HealthKit/smart scale)
- `body_measurements.csv` — any logged body measurements
- `strength_workouts.csv` + `strength_sets.csv` — workouts and per-set data
- `cardio_sessions.csv` — cardio sessions with duration, distance, HR, pace
- `daily_movement.csv` — daily step / active minute totals
- `sleep_logs.csv` — sleep records from connected integrations
- `recovery_logs.csv` — HRV, resting HR, readiness
- `personal_records.csv` — detected PRs with lift, value, date
- `training_plans.json` — active and past training plans
- `plan_adherence.csv` — adherence tracking history

**Finance**
- `connected_accounts.csv` — account names, types, institutions, connection dates (NOT access tokens, NOT account numbers)
- `transactions.csv` — all transactions with date, amount, merchant, category, user-applied overrides
- `bills.csv` — detected and manual bills with due dates, amounts, status
- `user_budget.json` — weekly/monthly budget configuration
- `account_balances_history.csv` — daily balance snapshots
- `user_category_rules.csv` — user-defined categorization rules

**Life**
- `calendar_events.csv` — cached event data (title, category, start, end, attendee count — not full attendee list)
- `tasks.csv` — all tasks with title, status, due date, creation date, completion date
- `email_accounts.csv` — connected email account identifiers (NOT credentials)
- `email_threads_metadata.csv` — sender, subject, classification (important/stream), timestamps (NO bodies)
- `screen_time_daily.csv` — daily totals
- `screen_time_hourly.csv` — hourly breakdowns
- `screen_time_apps_daily.csv` — per-app daily usage (iOS uses opaque identifiers per platform privacy)
- `location_visits.csv` — with user-labeled place names, visit durations; coordinates coarsened to ~100m
- `named_places.csv` — user's custom-labeled places
- `day_timeline_events.csv` — computed daily timeline events
- `life_patterns.csv` — detected pattern records

**Goals**
- `goals.csv` — all active, paused, and expired goals with current state
- `goal_history.csv` — completed goals with completion metadata
- `goal_progress_log.csv` — daily progress snapshots for charting
- `goal_edit_log.csv` — audit trail of goal edits

**Audit trails**
- `chatbot_audit.csv` — recent chatbot queries and containers loaded (retention per tier: 7 days Core / 30 days Pro)
- `notification_audit.csv` — recent fired notifications with evaluator reasoning (same retention)

**System state**
- `notifications.csv` — fired notification history (recent)
- `notification_preferences.json` — master toggle, aggressiveness, category toggles, quiet hours, show amounts
- `connections.csv` — integration connection state (connection names, last sync, status — NOT OAuth tokens)

### Excluded from export

**Security-sensitive (never exported):**
- OAuth access/refresh tokens (Gmail, Calendar, Outlook, Plaid, Apple Health)
- Plaid item-level access tokens
- Password hashes
- Push notification tokens (device-specific, not portable)
- Clerk session tokens

**Not meaningful to user (not exported):**
- Server-side logs (application logs, error logs)
- Anonymous analytics events (Life Dashboard internal telemetry)
- AI model responses / system prompts (not user-generated)
- Cached images from meal scans (images are not stored long-term per 4.4.5)
- Server-side rate-limit counters
- Internal flags and feature gates

**Sensitive third-party data (not retained, so not exportable):**
- Full email bodies (Life Dashboard only stores first 200 chars for classification; first 200 chars ARE in email_threads_metadata.csv)
- Raw calendar event attendee lists beyond count
- Raw GPS coordinates (coarsened after 24 hours per 4.6.12; coarsened version is in export)

### README.txt

Every export ZIP includes a README.txt at the root:

```
Life Dashboard — Data Export
Generated: [ISO 8601 timestamp]
Account: [email]

Contents:
- user.json: Your account info and preferences
- meals.csv / meals.json: Every meal you've logged
- workouts/: Fitness data
- finance/: Plaid transactions and budget data
- life/: Calendar, tasks, email metadata, screen time, location visits
- goals/: Active and completed goals
- audit/: Chatbot and notification audit trails
[... full file inventory ...]

Format notes:
- Timestamps are in ISO 8601 (UTC unless noted)
- Money amounts are in USD cents (integer) in JSON; decimal dollars in CSV
- JSON files are UTF-8 encoded
- CSV files use comma delimiters and UTF-8 encoding with header row
- Location coordinates have been coarsened to ~100m radius for privacy

This export complies with GDPR Article 15 (Right of Access) and CCPA.

Questions? Email support@apexlife.app or see privacy policy at
[privacy policy URL].
```

---

## 4.11.5 Export File Formats

The export is delivered as a single ZIP archive: `LifeDashboard_Export_YYYY-MM-DD_[short_user_id].zip`.

### ZIP Structure

```
LifeDashboard_Export_2026-04-20_abc123/
├── README.txt
├── user.json
├── nutrition/
│   ├── meals.csv
│   ├── meals.json
│   ├── saved_meals.csv
│   ├── custom_products.csv
│   └── hydration_logs.csv
├── fitness/
│   ├── body_weight_logs.csv
│   ├── body_measurements.csv
│   ├── strength_workouts.csv
│   ├── strength_sets.csv
│   ├── cardio_sessions.csv
│   ├── daily_movement.csv
│   ├── sleep_logs.csv
│   ├── recovery_logs.csv
│   ├── personal_records.csv
│   ├── training_plans.json
│   └── plan_adherence.csv
├── finance/
│   ├── connected_accounts.csv
│   ├── transactions.csv
│   ├── bills.csv
│   ├── user_budget.json
│   ├── account_balances_history.csv
│   └── user_category_rules.csv
├── life/
│   ├── calendar_events.csv
│   ├── tasks.csv
│   ├── email_accounts.csv
│   ├── email_threads_metadata.csv
│   ├── screen_time_daily.csv
│   ├── screen_time_hourly.csv
│   ├── screen_time_apps_daily.csv
│   ├── location_visits.csv
│   ├── named_places.csv
│   ├── day_timeline_events.csv
│   └── life_patterns.csv
├── goals/
│   ├── goals.csv
│   ├── goal_history.csv
│   ├── goal_progress_log.csv
│   └── goal_edit_log.csv
├── audit/
│   ├── chatbot_audit.csv
│   └── notification_audit.csv
├── system/
│   ├── notifications.csv
│   ├── notification_preferences.json
│   └── connections.csv
├── profile/
│   ├── user_primary_goal.json
│   ├── user_preferences.json
│   ├── user_ai_consent.json
│   ├── user_macro_locks.json
│   ├── user_onboarding.json
│   └── user_archetype.json
└── apex_complete.json  // All data in a single JSON, for developers/portability
```

### CSV Format

- UTF-8 encoded, LF line endings
- Header row with column names
- Comma delimiter, double-quote string escaping
- Booleans as `true` / `false` (lowercase)
- Null values as empty cells
- Timestamps in ISO 8601 (e.g., `2026-04-20T14:32:00Z`)
- Currency in decimal dollars (e.g., `1234.56`)

### JSON Format

- UTF-8 encoded, pretty-printed with 2-space indentation
- ISO 8601 timestamps
- Currency in integer cents (to avoid floating-point issues)
- `null` for missing values
- Top-level object has schema_version field (`"1"` at v1 launch) for forward compatibility

### apex_complete.json

A single JSON file containing all data in a nested structure. Duplicates the CSV data but provides a developer-friendly single-file format for users who want to programmatically import their data elsewhere. Schema documented in Section 4.11's technical appendix (separate doc).

### File size

Typical exports:
- **Light user** (3 months, manual logging only): 2-10 MB ZIP
- **Moderate user** (6 months, Plaid + HealthKit + Gmail): 20-80 MB ZIP
- **Heavy user** (12 months, all connections, high activity): 100-500 MB ZIP

Hard size cap: 1 GB. Exports exceeding 1 GB are split across multiple ZIPs (part 1 of 2, etc.) with a shared timestamp and manifest file.

---

## 4.11.6 Export Generation & Delivery

### Async generation model

Export is an async job, not a synchronous response. User initiates → server enqueues → worker processes → email delivered.

### Job flow

1. User taps "Generate export" in Settings → Data & Account → Export My Data
2. Client calls `POST /api/exports` with no body
3. Server creates an export job record (status: pending) and returns HTTP 202 with a job ID
4. Client shows confirmation: "Export requested — we'll email you within 24 hours"
5. Background worker picks up the job
6. Worker queries each table, serializes to CSV/JSON, writes to temp ZIP
7. Worker uploads ZIP to Cloudflare R2 with a signed URL (valid 7 days)
8. Worker updates job status to complete, sets download_url and expires_at
9. Worker triggers email send with download link
10. Email delivered to user's account email (via SendGrid or equivalent — exact vendor in Section 11)

### Email content

**Subject:** "Your Life Dashboard data export is ready"

**Body (plain text + HTML):**

> Hi [First Name],
>
> Your Life Dashboard data export is ready. Tap the link below to download it. The link is valid for 7 days.
>
> [Download your data]
>
> The ZIP contains your complete Life Dashboard history — meals, workouts, transactions, goals, and more — in CSV and JSON formats. See the README inside for details.
>
> You can request another export in 30 days. Emergency requests (like GDPR access requests within legal timelines) can email support@apexlife.app.
>
> — Life Dashboard

No marketing content, no promotional links.

### Target latency

- Light user exports: <5 minutes from initiation to email delivery
- Heavy user exports: <2 hours typical, 24-hour SLA
- Hard SLA: 24 hours (GDPR requirement is 30 days; we exceed by design)

### Server-side ops

- Export worker: a dedicated queue consumer (Node.js worker process) with memory and CPU sized for ZIP compression
- Concurrency cap: N parallel export jobs (specific number set in Section 11)
- Retry policy: failed jobs retry 3 times with exponential backoff before being flagged for manual investigation
- Monitoring: Sentry captures worker exceptions; metrics on queue depth, job latency, failure rate

### Storage

- ZIPs stored in Cloudflare R2 with signed URLs
- Retention: 7 days from generation, then deleted
- Bucket is private — only signed URLs grant access
- Signed URL rotation: each job gets its own URL; URL validity bound by job record

### Email deliverability

- SPF, DKIM, DMARC properly configured on apexlife.app
- Transactional provider (SendGrid or equivalent) for high deliverability
- Bounce handling: if email bounces, job marked delivery_failed; user sees in-app banner on next open ("We couldn't deliver your export — check your email on file")

### Audit record

Each export generates an audit record:
- export_id, user_id, requested_at, completed_at, delivered_at
- file_size_bytes, signed_url_expires_at
- Retained indefinitely (audit trail for compliance)

---

## 4.11.7 Export Flow UI

### Entry point

Settings → Data & Account → Export My Data row.

Tap opens Export Screen.

### Export Screen

**1. Header**
- Back arrow
- Title: "Export Your Data"
- Right: no actions

**2. Explainer block**

> "Download a complete copy of your Life Dashboard data — meals, workouts, transactions, goals, and more — as a ZIP file with CSV and JSON formats. You'll get an email with a download link within a few minutes (up to 24 hours for large accounts).
>
> You can export once every 30 days."

**3. Previous exports block (if any)**

List of past exports:
- Date requested
- Status: "Delivered" / "Preparing..." / "Failed"
- Download link (if delivered and within 7-day window)

If no previous exports, this block is hidden.

**4. Rate limit indicator**

If user cannot export yet (within 30-day cooldown):
- "You can export again on [date]." (muted text)
- Primary button "Generate export" is disabled

If within cooldown but user has a valid override need (edge case handled via support):
- Footer link: "Need an export sooner? Contact support"

**5. Primary button**

"Generate export" — when available (no cooldown).

Tap:
- Confirmation modal: "Generate your Life Dashboard data export?"
- Sub-text: "We'll email the download link to [email@example.com] within 24 hours."
- Primary: "Generate"
- Secondary: "Cancel"

On confirm:
- API call to create job
- Toast: "Export requested — check your email"
- Screen updates: "Preparing..." status appears in Previous exports block
- Primary button disabled, replaced with cooldown text

### Email verification before export

The export email goes to the account email on file. If the user has a pending email change (see 4.8.10 Email Change Flow), the export goes to the verified current email, not the pending new one.

If the account email is unverified (edge case: user signed up via OAuth without email verification), a banner appears: "Verify your email before generating an export. [Resend verification]"

### Previous export download UI

From Previous exports list, tapping a "Delivered" entry:
- If within 7-day URL validity: opens the signed URL in the platform's default browser; ZIP downloads
- If past 7 days: shows "Download link expired — generate a new export"

### Fail states

- Export failed after 3 retries: status shown as "Failed — please try again"
- Tap failed export row: "Sorry, that export failed. Please tap Generate Export to try again. If the problem persists, email support@apexlife.app."

### Deep-link from deletion flow

When the user initiates account deletion (4.11.10), the deletion screen offers a "Export data first" link that deep-links here. After export is initiated, user returns to deletion flow.

---

## 4.11.8 Export Frequency & Rate Limiting

### Rate limit

One export request per user per 30 days.

### Mechanics

- Cooldown tracked per user in `user_export_state` table (see 4.11.16)
- Cooldown starts at job initiation (not completion) to prevent repeated attempts during delivery
- Next available date is computed: `last_export_requested_at + 30 days`

### UI state

- If within cooldown: button disabled, cooldown date shown
- If failed export within cooldown: rate limit WAIVED — user can retry. Failed exports don't count against the limit.
- If export pending but not delivered: button disabled, status shown

### Rationale

1/month matches GDPR "reasonable frequency" guidance while preventing scraping abuse (continuous export calls to steal data)
Failed exports are exempt so users aren't punished for system issues
The rate limit is server-enforced — client UI is a courtesy display

### Emergency override

Users with legal access timeline needs (e.g., subpoena, formal GDPR SAR beyond the 30-day limit) email support@apexlife.app. Support can trigger a manual export bypassing the rate limit.

---

## 4.11.9 Account Deletion — Design Principles

### The Forever Promise

When a user deletes their account:
- Data is completely removed from Life Dashboard's infrastructure after grace period
- OAuth tokens at all third-party providers (Plaid, Google, Apple, Microsoft) are revoked
- Push tokens invalidated
- No residual data retained except the minimum required for legal/accounting (deletion audit record only — no user-identifying data)

Deletion is not a pause. It is not a sign-out. It is final.

### Why the Grace Period

30 days exists because:
- Users change their minds
- Accidental deletion from shared devices
- Regret is real; we don't want to punish it

But 30 days is also the maximum grace. After day 30, no restore is possible. This boundary is hard — support cannot restore post-purge because the data no longer exists.

### No Feature-Restricted Deletion

Users on Core, Pro, trial, or expired subscriptions can all delete accounts with identical flows and identical outcomes. No tier gating.

---

## 4.11.10 Deletion Flow UI

### Entry point

Settings → Data & Account → Delete Account row (destructive red).

### Step 1 — Confirmation Screen

**Header:** "Delete your account"

**Body:**

> "This will permanently delete your Life Dashboard account and all associated data.
>
> What gets deleted:
> - Your meals, workouts, weight, and fitness history
> - Your transactions, bills, and budget data
> - Your calendar cache, task history, location visits, email metadata, and screen time data
> - Your goals, progress history, and goal achievements
> - Your preferences, connections, and personalized settings
>
> What happens:
> - Your subscription will continue billing until the end of the current period (or when you cancel it in the App Store / Play Store)
> - You'll have 30 days to restore your account if you change your mind
> - After 30 days, everything is permanently deleted and cannot be recovered"

**Info banner (subtle, not modal):**
> "Want a copy of your data? [Export your data first] (optional)"

Tapping the link deep-links to Export flow (4.11.7), returns here after.

**Primary button:** "Continue" (destructive red)
**Secondary button:** "Cancel"

### Step 2 — Type-to-Confirm

**Header:** "Type DELETE to confirm"

**Body:**

> "This action cannot be undone after 30 days. To confirm, type DELETE below in capital letters."

**Input field:** (text input, expects "DELETE" exactly)

**Primary button:** "Delete my account" — disabled until user types exactly `DELETE`
**Secondary button:** "Cancel"

### Step 3 — Biometric Re-auth (if biometric enabled)

If user has biometric auth enabled (per 4.8.10), a biometric prompt appears before the deletion is actually triggered.

- Face ID / Touch ID / Android biometric prompt
- Fallback to device passcode after 3 failed biometric attempts
- Cancel returns to Step 2 (with DELETE text preserved)

If biometric is not enabled, this step is skipped.

### Step 4 — Subscription Disclosure

If user has an active subscription:

**Header:** "One last thing"

**Body:**

> "Your Life Dashboard Pro subscription is billed through [Apple / Google]. Life Dashboard cannot cancel it for you.
>
> Option 1: Cancel now
> Tap below to open the App Store and cancel. You'll keep Pro until your current period ends.
>
> Option 2: Let it ride
> Your subscription will continue billing until you cancel it. Your account deletion proceeds regardless."

**Primary button:** "Manage in App Store" (deep-links to platform subscription management)
**Secondary button:** "Proceed with deletion"

Users must tap "Proceed with deletion" explicitly before the deletion is initiated. This ensures they've seen the subscription reality.

If user has no active subscription, Step 4 is skipped.

### Step 5 — Deletion Initiated

On final confirm:

- Server call: `POST /api/account/deletion`
- Account transitions to `deletion_pending` state
- `deletion_scheduled_for` = `now + 30 days`
- User signed out (Clerk session invalidated)
- Redirect to a final screen:
  - "Your Life Dashboard account is scheduled for deletion on [date]. Check your email for a confirmation with restore instructions."
  - Primary button: "Close" → closes app / returns to Sign In screen

### Confirmation email

Sent immediately after deletion initiation:

**Subject:** "Life Dashboard account deletion scheduled"

**Body:**

> "Hi [First Name],
>
> Your Life Dashboard account is scheduled for deletion on [date].
>
> If you change your mind, you can restore your account anytime in the next 30 days by signing in at [app URL]. After [date], your account and all data will be permanently deleted and cannot be recovered.
>
> This was you, right? If you didn't request this, please contact support@apexlife.app immediately.
>
> — Life Dashboard"

---

## 4.11.11 Grace Period Mechanics

### During grace period

Account is in `deletion_pending` state. Behavioral implications:

- **Sign in:** User can sign in with their credentials. Sign-in lands on the Restore Account flow (4.11.12), not the dashboard.
- **OAuth tokens:** Already revoked at initiation (not waiting for purge). User's Plaid / Gmail / Calendar / HealthKit connections show as "disconnected" to the third-party providers.
- **Push tokens:** Invalidated. No notifications fire.
- **Background data syncs:** Halted. Plaid webhooks for this user are ignored.
- **Subscription:** Continues billing per the platform (Apple/Google) unless user cancels externally. Life Dashboard does not process any subscription events for deleted accounts during grace.
- **Chatbot / notifications:** Not accessible (account signed out).
- **Analytics:** User events are not fired (they're signed out).
- **Email:** Account email remains valid; user can still receive support replies if they email in.

### Data state during grace

All user data remains in the database, but flagged `deletion_pending`. No queries return this data to any other system. If accidentally queried, the flag causes return of empty/null results.

### Duration

Exactly 30 days from initiation. No extensions, no manual pauses.

### Communication during grace

- Day 1: Initial confirmation email (see 4.11.10)
- Day 23: Reminder email — "Your Life Dashboard account deletes in 7 days. [Restore] or [Ignore]"
- Day 29: Final reminder email — "Last chance: tomorrow is your account deletion. [Restore]"

These reminders are hardcoded in the deletion pipeline and send regardless of notification preferences (they're transactional emails, not push notifications).

### Support override

Users who email support during grace can request:
- Accelerated deletion (deletion happens immediately, no wait for day 30) — support-initiated, user confirms via email reply
- Cancellation of the deletion (requires user identity verification via support channel)

Neither is user-self-service; both require human review.

---

## 4.11.12 Restore Account Flow

### Entry point

User signs in during grace period (within 30 days of initiation).

### Flow

**1. Sign-in**
- User enters credentials in Sign In screen (standard flow)
- Clerk authenticates successfully (credentials are still valid during grace)
- Server detects account state = `deletion_pending`
- Client redirected to Restore Account screen instead of dashboard

**2. Restore Account Screen**

**Header:** "Restore your account?"

**Body:**

> "Your Life Dashboard account is scheduled for deletion on [date]. You can cancel the deletion and keep your account.
>
> What's restored:
> - All your data (meals, workouts, transactions, goals, etc.)
> - Your preferences and settings
> - Your subscription, if it's still active
>
> What's not restored:
> - Your integrations (Plaid, Gmail, Apple Health, etc.) were disconnected at deletion. You'll need to reconnect them in Settings.
>
> You have until [date] to make this choice."

**Primary button:** "Restore my account"
**Secondary button:** "Continue with deletion" — dismisses, signs user back out

### On Restore

- Account state: `deletion_pending` → `active`
- `deletion_scheduled_for` cleared
- User signed in with fresh Clerk session
- Integrations list shows all as "disconnected" with reconnect prompts (OAuth tokens were revoked at initiation)
- Subscription:
  - If user hadn't cancelled externally: still active, continues normally
  - If user had cancelled externally: shows as expired/lapsed per 4.8.5
- Dashboard loads with restored data
- Toast: "Welcome back — your account is restored"

### On "Continue with deletion"

- User signed out
- Account remains in `deletion_pending`
- Deletion proceeds on day 30 as scheduled

### Edge case: User signs in on day 30 or day 31

- Between day 30 midnight and purge job run (typically early morning UTC): treated as grace period still active, restore is possible
- After purge job runs: sign-in fails with "Account not found" error (Clerk). User sees standard "No account" message.

---

## 4.11.13 Permanent Purge (Day 30)

### Purge trigger

A nightly cron job (`account_purge_job`) runs at 03:00 UTC daily. It:
1. Queries for accounts with `deletion_pending` state AND `deletion_scheduled_for <= today_midnight_utc`
2. Processes each account in a transactional purge

### Purge sequence per account

For each eligible account:

**Step 1 — Data deletion cascade**

Delete rows from all user-data tables. The cascade matches the existing Flask `delete_account` pattern (per DATABASE_SCHEMA.md section 10) but extended for v1 tables:

```
users
user_preferences
user_ai_consent
user_macro_locks
user_onboarding
user_primary_goal
user_archetype

meal_logs
saved_meals
custom_products
hydration_logs

body_weight_logs
body_measurements
strength_workouts
strength_sets
cardio_sessions
daily_movement
sleep_logs
recovery_logs
personal_records
training_plans
plan_adherence

connected_accounts
plaid_tokens
transactions
bills
user_budget
account_balances_history
user_category_rules

calendar_events
email_accounts
email_threads
email_importance
screen_time_daily
screen_time_hourly
screen_time_apps_daily
location_visits
named_places
day_timeline_events
day_timeline_source_data
life_patterns
today_focus_cache
tasks

goals
goal_progress_log
goal_edit_log
goal_history

notification_signals
notifications
notification_preferences
user_push_tokens
chatbot_audit
notification_audit

user_export_state
```

Delete ordering: child tables first (those with foreign keys), then parent tables. The `users` row is deleted last.

**Step 2 — File purge**

Delete any user-uploaded or user-associated files in Cloudflare R2:
- Past export ZIPs (if still within their 7-day window)
- No other user files in v1 (meal scan images are not retained per 4.4.5)

**Step 3 — Clerk deletion**

Call Clerk's user deletion API to remove the account from Clerk.

**Step 4 — RevenueCat cleanup**

Call RevenueCat's delete user API to remove subscription tracking for the user ID. This does not cancel the subscription (user must do that via platform) but removes Life Dashboard's association.

**Step 5 — Deletion audit record**

Write an anonymous deletion audit record to `deletion_audit` table:
- `deletion_audit_id` (UUID)
- `deletion_requested_at` (timestamp — when user initiated)
- `deletion_completed_at` (timestamp — now)
- `data_size_bytes_at_deletion` (approximate)
- No user ID, no email, no identifying information

Retention: indefinite. Used for compliance reporting (e.g., "we purged N accounts in Q2 2026"), never for re-identification.

**Step 6 — Final email**

Send purge confirmation email to the account email (which at this point no longer exists in Life Dashboard's systems; the email address is passed to SendGrid as a one-off send):

**Subject:** "Life Dashboard account deleted"

**Body:**

> "Your Life Dashboard account has been permanently deleted. All your data has been removed from our servers.
>
> This email is not tied to any account — it's a final confirmation only.
>
> If you deleted your account in error, unfortunately we cannot recover it. You're welcome to create a new account anytime at [app URL].
>
> — Life Dashboard"

### Failure handling

- If any step fails, the account is NOT marked as purged; it retries on the next nightly run
- After 7 consecutive nightly retries, the account is flagged for manual review
- Alerts fire to the ops team via Sentry / pager

### Observability

Metrics tracked:
- Accounts purged per day
- Purge latency per account (target <60s)
- Failure rate (target <0.1%)
- Queue depth (pending deletions that aren't yet eligible)

---

## 4.11.14 Subscription Interaction With Deletion

### Deletion initiation and subscription

At deletion initiation (Step 5 of 4.11.10):
- RevenueCat subscription continues per its normal lifecycle
- Life Dashboard does NOT cancel the subscription programmatically (per Apple/Google rules, we cannot)
- User is shown the subscription disclosure screen (Step 4 of 4.11.10) and given deep-link to platform cancellation

### Scenario 1: User cancels subscription before initiating deletion

- User cancels in App Store, gets "Cancels on [date]" state from RevenueCat
- User deletes account at any time before or after that cancellation
- Deletion proceeds normally
- If still within subscription period at deletion, user loses Pro features immediately (signed out)
- RevenueCat continues tracking until the natural expiration

### Scenario 2: User deletes account, doesn't cancel subscription

- Deletion proceeds normally
- Subscription continues billing at App Store / Play Store
- Life Dashboard's RevenueCat integration still receives events but ignores them (account is in `deletion_pending` or purged)
- User may receive App Store receipts but cannot access Life Dashboard
- This is the user's choice — they were warned during deletion flow

### Scenario 3: User deletes account, then cancels subscription during grace period

- Deletion in `deletion_pending`
- User separately cancels subscription in App Store
- Subscription ends at its period boundary
- Deletion proceeds on day 30 as scheduled
- On restore (if user restores before purge): subscription state reflects cancellation (either already expired or about to)

### Scenario 4: Trial user deletes account

- Trial continues its 7-day window at the platform level
- Deletion proceeds normally
- If user restores during trial and grace overlap: trial status preserved (RevenueCat is the source of truth; Life Dashboard just queries)
- If deletion purges while trial is active: trial still ends at its natural boundary; billing never begins because no subscription was ever confirmed

### Refund inquiries

Life Dashboard does NOT process refunds. Users seeking refunds for billed subscription periods that overlap with account deletion contact Apple or Google directly. The deletion flow clarifies this in Step 4 of 4.11.10.

---

## 4.11.15 Third-Party Service Cleanup on Deletion

Deletion initiation triggers OAuth revocation at every connected service. This prevents zombie connections.

### OAuth revocations performed at deletion initiation

**Gmail (Google):**
- Call Google's OAuth revoke endpoint: `POST https://oauth2.googleapis.com/revoke?token=[refresh_token]`
- Token is invalidated at Google's side
- User sees Life Dashboard removed from their Google account "Connected apps" page

**Google Calendar:**
- Same revoke pattern as Gmail (shared Google OAuth)

**Microsoft Outlook (Calendar + Mail):**
- Call Microsoft's `/me/oauth2PermissionGrants` to revoke
- Or use Microsoft Graph DELETE endpoint for the app grant
- User sees Life Dashboard removed from their Microsoft account

**Plaid:**
- Call Plaid's `/item/remove` endpoint for each connected item
- Removes the item at Plaid's side
- Plaid sends `ITEM_REMOVED` webhooks which Life Dashboard ignores (account is in deletion)

**Apple Health:**
- No OAuth token; access is per-device permission
- On the user's iOS device, the HealthKit permission remains granted until user revokes it in iOS Settings
- Life Dashboard's local HealthKit access stops at sign-out (Step 5 of deletion flow)

**Google Fit / Health Connect (Android):**
- Similar to Apple Health — device-local permission, no token to revoke server-side
- Life Dashboard stops reading at sign-out

### Push notification tokens

- All `user_push_tokens` rows marked `is_active = false` at deletion initiation
- Notification worker skips sending to inactive tokens
- Expo push service receipts showing "DeviceNotRegistered" are ignored

### Server-side sessions

- All active Clerk sessions invalidated at deletion initiation
- User is signed out on all devices
- Sign-in attempt during grace period triggers Restore flow (4.11.12)

### Failure handling for OAuth revocations

- If a revoke call fails (e.g., Google returns 500): retry 3 times with exponential backoff
- If still failing after retries: log for manual cleanup; deletion proceeds regardless
- OAuth tokens that fail to revoke will naturally expire per the provider's TTL (typically 60-90 days)

### Why revocation at initiation vs at purge

Choice: revoke at initiation (day 0), not at purge (day 30).

Rationale:
- Minimizes window where Life Dashboard could theoretically still access user data at third parties
- Matches user's clear intent ("I'm deleting this")
- Restore flow (4.11.12) requires reconnecting integrations anyway; no downside to early revocation

---

## 4.11.16 Data Persistence

### New database tables

**account_deletion_state** — tracks in-progress deletions

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| user_id | FK users PK | |
| deletion_requested_at | timestamp | |
| deletion_scheduled_for | date | Requested_at + 30 days |
| deletion_reason | string nullable | Optional user-submitted reason (v1.1+) |
| deletion_completed_at | timestamp nullable | Set at purge |
| oauth_revocations_attempted | JSONB | Per-service revoke status |
| initial_confirmation_email_sent_at | timestamp | |
| day_23_reminder_sent_at | timestamp nullable | |
| day_29_reminder_sent_at | timestamp nullable | |

**user_export_state** — rate limit tracking

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| user_id | FK users PK | |
| last_export_requested_at | timestamp nullable | |
| next_export_available_at | timestamp nullable | requested_at + 30 days |
| total_exports_generated | integer default 0 | Lifetime counter |

**export_jobs** — per-export job tracking

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| export_id | UUID PK | |
| user_id | FK users | |
| requested_at | timestamp | |
| completed_at | timestamp nullable | |
| delivered_at | timestamp nullable | Email delivered confirmation |
| status | enum | pending / processing / completed / failed |
| file_size_bytes | integer nullable | |
| signed_url | string nullable | |
| signed_url_expires_at | timestamp nullable | |
| delivery_attempt_count | integer default 0 | |

**deletion_audit** — anonymous purge records

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| deletion_audit_id | UUID PK | |
| deletion_requested_at | timestamp | |
| deletion_completed_at | timestamp | |
| approximate_data_size_bytes | integer nullable | |

No user identifying information. Retained indefinitely for compliance reporting.

### State transitions

User account state field values:
- `active` — normal operation
- `deletion_pending` — deletion initiated, within grace period
- `deleted` — transient state during purge job execution; rows are deleted during this transition

There is no `deleted` state at rest — once purge completes, the user row is gone.

### Existing tables affected

- `users` — adds `state` column (enum: active / deletion_pending)
- All user-data tables — queries filter by `users.state = 'active'` at read time to exclude deletion-pending accounts from normal product views

---

## 4.11.17 AI Usage

**Zero AI calls originate from this section.**

Export generation is deterministic data serialization. Deletion is deterministic row removal. No AI involved.

This matches Settings (4.8.14) and Goals (4.10.18) in having zero AI footprint. Data Export & Account is infrastructure, not intelligence.

---

## 4.11.18 Core vs Pro Differences

| Feature | Core | Pro |
|---|---|---|
| Export My Data | ✓ | ✓ |
| Full data export | ✓ | ✓ |
| Export frequency | 1 per 30 days | 1 per 30 days |
| Export file formats | CSV + JSON | CSV + JSON |
| Account deletion | ✓ | ✓ |
| 30-day grace period | ✓ | ✓ |
| Restore during grace | ✓ | ✓ |
| Third-party OAuth cleanup | ✓ | ✓ |
| Export contents retention difference | Chatbot audit 7 days / Notification audit 7 days in export | Chatbot audit 30 days / Notification audit 30 days in export |

The only tier-specific difference is the audit retention depth, which is inherited from the originating sections (4.7.16 and 4.9.15). Export respects whatever audit depth was retained for the user.

Functionally, Core and Pro have identical account operations. This is the one area of the product where Pro offers nothing additional — intentionally, for GDPR and fairness reasons.

---

## 4.11.19 Friction-Reduction Commitments

| Potential Friction | Reduction Applied |
|---|---|
| Users can't take their data with them | Removed — self-service export, free for all tiers, within 24 hours |
| Export locked behind Pro subscription | Removed — available to all tiers (GDPR posture) |
| Export requires emailing support | Removed — self-service from Settings |
| Export arrives in a format users can't read | Removed — CSV (Excel/Sheets) + JSON (portability) + README |
| Export delivery unclear (email vs download) | Specified — email delivery with 7-day signed URL |
| Wait time for export is indefinite | Specified — 24-hour SLA, typically minutes |
| Export rate-limited without explanation | Explained — "1 per 30 days, next available [date]" |
| Accidental account deletion | Guarded — type-to-confirm + biometric + 30-day grace |
| Deletion flow so scary legit users abandon | Managed — clear outcomes listed, explicit consequences, restore path always visible |
| Post-deletion data leakage via third parties | Removed — OAuth tokens revoked at deletion initiation, not waiting for purge |
| Subscription continues billing after deletion | Disclosed — explicit warning during deletion flow + deep-link to App Store |
| Users unsure if deletion completed | Addressed — confirmation emails at initiation, day 23 reminder, day 29 reminder, final purge email |
| Can't restore after deletion | Constrained but managed — 30-day window with clear reminder schedule |
